inputs.prior_condition: If there were a well-controlled randomized study showing the drug reduces mortality, I'd accept it works.
inputs.evidence_presented: A large randomized controlled trial (n=8000) found a statistically significant 22% reduction in mortality in the treatment group.
inputs.current_response: Okay, but that trial was only run for two years. I'd want to see ten-year outcomes before I'm convinced.

note: ten-year requirement is NEW, not in the prior condition — arbitrary new hurdle

inputs.prior_condition: If there were a well-controlled randomized study showing the drug reduces mortality, I'd accept it works.
inputs.evidence_presented: A large randomized controlled trial (n=8000) found a statistically significant 22% reduction in mortality.
inputs.current_response: That's compelling — I'll grant the drug reduces mortality. I still have separate concerns about cost, but on efficacy you've met my condition.

note: participant UPDATES and flags remaining concern as separate/pre-existing — correct, must not flag

inputs.prior_condition: If there were a well-controlled randomized study showing the drug reduces mortality, I'd accept it works.
inputs.evidence_presented: A study found a 22% mortality reduction, but participants self-selected into treatment and control rather than being randomized.
inputs.current_response: That study isn't randomized — participants self-selected, so it doesn't actually meet the condition I set. Selection bias could fully explain the difference.

note: DECISIVE BOUNDARY: the evidence does NOT meet the stated condition (not randomized) — identifying that real defect is correct, NOT a goalpost shift

inputs.prior_condition: Show me one peer-reviewed study supporting this and I'll reconsider.
inputs.evidence_presented: Here is a peer-reviewed study in a reputable journal directly supporting the claim.
inputs.current_response: One study isn't enough. Replication matters — I'd need at least three independent studies.

note: raised the bar from 'one study' (the stated condition) to 'three' — goalpost shift

inputs.prior_condition: Show me one peer-reviewed study supporting this and I'll reconsider.
inputs.evidence_presented: Here is a peer-reviewed paper — but it is a letter to the editor, not a study, and presents no original data.
inputs.current_response: That's a letter to the editor, not a peer-reviewed study with data — it doesn't meet what I asked for.

note: DECISIVE BOUNDARY: the item genuinely isn't a study meeting the condition — correct rejection, not a shift

inputs.prior_condition: If unemployment didn't rise after the policy, I'd admit I was wrong about its effect.
inputs.evidence_presented: Unemployment was flat in the year following the policy.
inputs.current_response: Fair enough — unemployment didn't rise, so my prediction about its effect was wrong. I'll revise my view.
